articles.title: China's Communist Party investigates ex-Xinjiang leader Ma Xingrui
articles.summary: Ma Xingrui is a member of the party's Central Committee and served as party secretary of the Xinjiang region in China's northwest from 2021-2025.

articles.title: Job Growth Rebounded in March
articles.summary: Payrolls expanded and unemployment dropped last month after a health care strike ended and a harsh winter abated.

articles.title: Hegseth Says U.S. Troops Are Fighting for Jesus. The Pope Disagrees.
articles.summary: In sharp contrast to the Trump administration’s calls for Christian prayers for the war effort, Pope Leo XIV says military domination is “entirely foreign to the way of Jesus Christ.”

articles.title: Pam Bondi Wanted a Graceful Exit. But Trump Wanted Her Gone.
articles.summary: Pam Bondi had a feeling her days as attorney general were numbered. But she didn’t expect President Trump to drop the curtain quite so soon.

articles.title: Seville, Spain's Holy Week blends faith, tradition and spectacle
articles.summary: Even as religious belief declines in Spain, the processions at Seville's Semana Santa — the Holy Week lead-up to Easter — draw crowds moved by music, tradition and powerful emotion.

articles.title: NASA Unveils 1st Earth Photos From Artemis II Moon Mission: ‘You Look Beautiful.’
articles.summary: The pictures were released on the third day of the first mission since 1972 to send people around the moon.

articles.title: Economists Are Drawing Stronger Connections Between A.I. and Jobs
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence hasn’t disrupted the labor market, economists say, but they are increasingly convinced that it will — and that policymakers are unprepared.

articles.title: A U.S. jet goes down over Iran, a U.S. official confirms
articles.summary: A U.S. official said that one crew member had been rescued and U.S. forces continue to search for the second crew member.

articles.title: Strong Jobs Numbers Make the Fed’s Job Easier
articles.summary: Robust job growth in March suggests that the labor market remains relatively healthy, allowing officials at the central bank to focus on fighting inflation.

articles.title: 30 Years Later, the Unabomber’s Cabin Is a Prized F.B.I. Artifact
articles.summary: A complicated piece of American heritage and culture sits intact in the F.B.I. headquarters.

articles.title: The labor market springs back to life in March as employers add 178,000 jobs
articles.summary: The U.S. job market perked up last month as employers added 178,000 jobs. The unemployment rate dipped to 4.3%, mainly because the number of people seeking work declined.

articles.title: Trump budget seeks $1.5 trillion in defense spending alongside domestic program cuts
articles.summary: In his annual budget, President Trump is asking Congress to boost defense spending to $1.5 trillion, the largest such request in decades.

articles.title: Pam Bondi is out at DOJ. And, NASA's Artemis II has left Earth's orbit
articles.summary: President Trump announced yesterday that Pam Bondi is out as Attorney General. And, NASA's Artemis II has left Earth's orbit and is heading toward the moon.

articles.title: Iran downs a U.S. F-15 jet and hits Gulf refineries as the war rounds its 5th week
articles.summary: Iran said one of the longest bridges linking Tehran to the city of Karaj was destroyed overnight, while Iranian missiles and drones hit Israel, Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates and Kuwait.

articles.title: Verdicts against Meta and Google may bring a new era of big tech accountability
articles.summary: Advocates hope recent verdicts against social media platforms will build momentum for bigger changes in Silicon Valley.

articles.title: 50 Artists Travel Far and Wide to Learn From Filmmaker Werner Herzog
articles.summary: In January, 50 artists from all over the world were chosen to shoot shorts in the Azores, guided by the iconic filmmaker. They just had to find $10,000 and airfare to get there.

articles.title: Evacuation of U.S. troops from Mideast base sends community groups scrambling to help
articles.summary: Troops and their families have been pushed back to the United States after their bases in the Middle East were threatened by Iranian counterattacks. Community groups are scrambling to react.

articles.title: Former U.S. ambassador talks about Trump's rhetoric on NATO
articles.summary: NPR's A Martinez asks Dan Baer, former U.S. ambassador to the Organization for Security and Cooperation in Europe, about the Trump administration's recent rhetoric around NATO.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz closure deflates global helium supply
articles.summary: The blockade in the Strait of Hormuz is affecting not only oil, but the global helium supply.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
